strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq — including MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — demonstrating hands-on, production-oriented AI product development that directly maps to the role's builder and AI fluency requirements.

17+ years of engineering breadth spanning embedded systems, high-throughput data pipelines (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker), and full-stack product development, satisfying the 15-year minimum with domain diversity that strengthens cross-functional advisory credibility.

Founder of GISCollective (active open-source geospatial platform since 2016), creator of multiple OSS libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Vibe.dav), and a documented public knowledge-sharer through FOSS4G conference talks and multi-day workshops — evidencing the entrepreneurial drive and ability to inspire others the role explicitly calls for.
concerns: No formal AI/ML research background, graduate credentials, or history of building ML models; the AI experience is recent (April 2025) and tooling-focused rather than strategy-level or customer-advisory in nature.
Career titles (Senior/Staff Software Developer) reflect an implementation and craft track rather than the director-level IC seniority the role targets, suggesting a meaningful leveling gap.

No demonstrated experience advising enterprise customers or strategic partners on cloud and AI adoption — the background is primarily product-building and open-source, not external strategy consulting or executive stakeholder engagement.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, current, hands-on credibility in AI agent infrastructure — he is actively shipping MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks in production today, not theorizing about them, which gives him the builder fluency the role prizes above all else. That technical edge is grounded in 17+ years of broad engineering across high-throughput systems, cloud infrastructure, embedded hardware, and open-source platform ownership, meeting and exceeding the experience threshold with meaningful domain diversity. His founder history at GISCollective and a track record of public workshops and conference talks demonstrate the capacity to inspire and share insights externally that the advisory dimension of this role requires. The primary stretch is the jump from Staff Engineer to director-level IC and the absence of formal AI strategy advisory experience with enterprise customers, but his current AI work at re:cinq provides a credible, up-to-date bridge that makes him worth a conversation.
